channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
item.content:encoded: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Former State Department official on the implications of the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Number of cyclosporiasis cases in the U.S. has surpassed previous records, CDC says
item.description: It can take weeks to confirm a single case of intestinal illness caused by the cyclospora parasite, but the CDC's tally for this year now stands at over 4,100.
item.content:encoded: It can take weeks to confirm a single case of intestinal illness caused by the cyclospora parasite, but the CDC's tally for this year now stands at over 4,100.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: DOT cranks up its campaign to strip bike lane references from federal websites
item.description: Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.
item.content:encoded:
Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
item.description: The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.
item.content:encoded:
The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump administration signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, Trump ramps up aggression against Iran despite declining support, House passes Pentagon funding bill.
item.content:encoded: Trump administration signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, Trump ramps up aggression against Iran despite declining support, House passes Pentagon funding bill.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Independent autopsy 'inconclusive' on cause of death for 18-year-old Mississippi teen
item.description: There are more questions after preliminary results of an independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells were inconclusive. The 18 year-old was left on a Mississippi island by friends.
item.content:encoded: There are more questions after preliminary results of an independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells were inconclusive. The 18 year-old was left on a Mississippi island by friends.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: A year after Trump funding cuts, public media finds support from audiences
item.description: A year ago, President Trump cut all federal funding for public TV and radio. Hundreds of public media staffers got laid off. But listeners and viewers are also stepping up with support.
item.content:encoded: A year ago, President Trump cut all federal funding for public TV and radio. Hundreds of public media staffers got laid off. But listeners and viewers are also stepping up with support.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Israel will hold elections this fall. Gadi Eisenkott could be the next prime minister
item.description: Gadi Eisenkott has emerged as a leading contender to be Israel's next prime minister and the strongest challenger to Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.content:encoded: Gadi Eisenkott has emerged as a leading contender to be Israel's next prime minister and the strongest challenger to Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
item.description: Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.
item.content:encoded:
Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone
